label: Sumo Logic
description: Connect to Sumo Logic to query logs, metrics, and security analytics using your Access ID and Access Key.

form.title: Connect Sumo Logic
form.subtitle: Paste your Sumo Logic Access ID and Access Key to start querying logs, metrics, and security data.

methods.label: Access ID & Access Key
methods.description: Paste a Sumo Logic Access ID and Access Key from Preferences → Access Keys. The simplest and most direct authentication path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Sumo Logic Access Key

Sumo Logic uses an Access ID + Access Key pair as credentials for API access. These are scoped to your user account.

### Steps

1. Log in to your Sumo Logic account.
2. Click your name or avatar in the top-right corner and select **Preferences**.
3. Scroll to the **Access Keys** section and click **Add Access Key**.
4. Give the key a descriptive name like `Anton` and click **Create**.
5. Sumo Logic displays your **Access ID** and **Access Key** — copy both immediately. The Access Key is shown only once.
6. Paste both values into the fields below and choose the **Region** that matches your Sumo Logic deployment (check the URL in your browser: e.g. `service.us2.sumologic.com` → `us2`).

### Notes

- The Access Key is shown only at creation time. If you lose it, delete the old key and create a new one.
- Access Keys inherit the permissions of the user who created them. Use a service account user if you want to restrict scope.
- If your org uses SAML SSO, an admin may need to allow access key creation — check **Administration → Security → Policies**.
- For role-based access control, ensure the user has at minimum the **View** permission on the data sources you want Anton to query.

fields.name: access_id
fields.label: Access ID
fields.description: Your Sumo Logic Access ID — found in Preferences → Access Keys at creation time.

fields.name: access_key
fields.label: Access Key
fields.description: Your Sumo Logic Access Key — shown only once when the key is created. If lost, delete and recreate it.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Region / Deployment
fields.description: Select the Sumo Logic deployment that matches your account URL (e.g. service.us2.sumologic.com → us2).
